description: Public-safe PII detection patterns. Generic shapes only - NO operator-specific literals. Operator-specific markers (personal/team email, private domain, confidential keyword lists) have been REMOVED from this public file and must be supplied at scan time from a gitignored private config (e.g. security/policy/.private-markers.json, never committed). Embedding operator literals here would itself leak them.
notes: Regexes target PCRE/RE2-compatible engines. Case-insensitivity is encoded inline with (?i) where intended. RE2 (Go and many scanners) does not support lookbehind/lookahead; where lookarounds are used, the description gives an RE2 fallback. Severity scale: low | medium | high | critical.

patterns.name: email_generic
patterns.description: Generic email address shape (local-part @ domain . tld). Catches most real addresses without enforcing full RFC 5322.

patterns.name: phone_e164
patterns.description: International phone in strict E.164: leading + then country code and up to 15 digits total. Lookarounds avoid matching inside longer digit runs; for RE2 drop the lookarounds and use \b anchors.

patterns.name: phone_international_formatted
patterns.description: International phone with separators, optional 00 IDD prefix or +, optional area-code parens, and grouped digits. Looser than E.164 to catch human-formatted numbers like +44 (0)20 7946 0958 or 00 91 98765 43210.

patterns.name: phone_na_local
patterns.description: North American local format without country code: (NXX) NXX-XXXX or NXX-NXX-XXXX. Medium severity because 10-digit local strings collide with other identifiers.

patterns.name: street_address_us_uk
patterns.description: Street address shape: house/building number then 1-4 street-name tokens then a common thoroughfare/unit keyword (US + UK vocabulary). Heuristic; may miss non-Latin or PO-box-only addresses.

patterns.name: postal_code_us_zip
patterns.description: US ZIP or ZIP+4. Low severity alone (5 digits are ambiguous); treat as PII mainly when co-located with an address or name.

patterns.name: postal_code_uk
patterns.description: UK postcode outward+inward shape, e.g. SW1A 1AA or EC1A1BB. Fairly specific, so medium severity.

patterns.name: name_with_context
patterns.description: Person name detected via a preceding context label or honorific (e.g. 'Name: Jane Doe', 'Attn: John Smith'). Captures 2-4 capitalized name tokens in group 1. Context-gated to reduce false positives on ordinary capitalized words.

patterns.name: name_honorific_inline
patterns.description: Honorific immediately followed by 1-3 capitalized tokens, e.g. 'Mr Smith', 'Dr. Jane Patel'. Complements name_with_context for inline prose.

patterns.name: govid_us_ssn
patterns.description: US Social Security Number AAA-GG-SSSS with invalid-area exclusions (no 000/666/9xx area, no 00 group, no 0000 serial). Dashed form only to limit false positives; see govid_us_ssn_nodash for the no-separator variant.

patterns.name: govid_us_ssn_nodash
patterns.description: US SSN without separators (9 consecutive digits with the same area/group/serial exclusions). High not critical because 9-digit runs are ambiguous with other IDs; pair with context for confidence.

patterns.name: govid_credit_card
patterns.description: Major credit-card PANs (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover, Diners) by IIN prefix and length. Validate matches with the Luhn checksum downstream to cut false positives.

patterns.name: govid_iban
patterns.description: IBAN bank account shape: 2-letter country, 2 check digits, then grouped alphanumerics (15-34 chars total). Validate length-per-country and mod-97 checksum downstream.

patterns.name: govid_us_passport
patterns.description: Passport number gated by the word 'passport' plus a 6-9 digit (optionally one leading letter) value in group 1. Context-gated because bare passport numbers are indistinguishable from many other IDs.

patterns.name: govid_generic_labeled
patterns.description: Catch-all for government IDs introduced by a label (national id, tax id, SSN, SIN, NINO, Aadhaar, PAN, driver's license, etc.) followed by an alphanumeric value in group 1.

patterns.name: ip_address_v4
patterns.description: IPv4 address with valid 0-255 octets. Low severity as a quasi-identifier; raise to medium when correlated with a user/session.
